spend_backfill_manual_week_end.csv_path: C:\Users\rusht\Downloads\KPI Sober Founders - Before I ran my own meta ads, manual import.csv
spend_backfill_manual_week_end.assumption: Labels are week-end dates; each value allocated evenly across interval since prior label (first label assumed 7-day interval). Blank cells treated as unknown and not allocated. Manual spend is additive to live Meta free-funnel spend, including overlap during transition.

checks.label: Exact membership tag "Main Free $250k+ Group" with cached official revenue 0/null
Any membership string containing "Main Free $250k+ Group" with cached official revenue 0/null
Cohort analyzed contacts with cached official revenue
Cohort analyzed contacts with sobriety date present
Observed great members (6+ show-ups) in current cohort range
Observed ideal members (11+ show-ups + ICP) in current cohort range
High-value nudge candidates (ICP profile, strong member, not yet ideal)
Strong non-ICP members (great attendance but currently fail ICP)
Great leads with manual outreach opportunity

weekly_signoff.summary: Provisional signoff: core math checks passed, but warnings remain (sample size / coverage / trend pressure). Use for decisions with explicit caveats.

methodology.cohort_unit: weekly (Monday UTC) based on HubSpot contact createdate for Meta-paid, non-Phoenix contacts
methodology.spend_source: raw_fb_ads_insights_daily filtered funnel_key=free + additive manual weekly free-funnel spend backfill (week-end labels allocated across intervals; blanks ignored as unknown)
methodology.showup_source: HubSpot call activities + contact associations (Tuesday/Thursday group sessions only)
methodology.luma_source: raw_luma_registrations approved rows (matched_hubspot_contact_id with email fallback)
methodology.preexisting_exclusion: Exclude contacts with first group show-up or Luma signup more than 14 days before lead createdate
methodology.quality_definition: official revenue field + sobriety >1y at lead date (current cached contact snapshot; no property history yet)
methodology.ideal_member_definition: 11+ group show-ups + revenue >=250k official + >1y sober at lead date

checks.label: Cohort lead count matches analyzed cohort row count
Sum of cohort-week leads equals analyzed cohort row count
Meta Free Lead finalized CPA formula check
Luma Signup (first approved registration) finalized CPA formula check
Luma Signup (first approved registration) projected CPA formula check
First Show-Up (HubSpot Calls Tue/Thu) finalized CPA formula check
First Show-Up (HubSpot Calls Tue/Thu) projected CPA formula check
Qualified Lead ($250k-$999,999 official + >1y sober at lead date) finalized CPA formula check
Great Lead (>= $1M official + >1y sober at lead date) finalized CPA formula check
Great Member (6+ show-ups) finalized CPA formula check
Great Member (6+ show-ups) projected CPA formula check
Ideal Member (11+ show-ups + ICP profile) finalized CPA formula check
Ideal Member (11+ show-ups + ICP profile) projected CPA formula check
Current CPL (Trailing 4 Cohort Weeks) drilldown math check
CPL (Trailing 12 Cohort Weeks) drilldown math check
Current-Entry Forecast CPQL drilldown math check
Current-Entry Forecast Cost / Great Lead drilldown math check
Current-Entry Forecast Cost / First Show-Up drilldown math check
Observed ideal-member achievers do not exceed great-member achievers
Official revenue coverage in cohort analyzed contacts
notes: Lower coverage can bias qualified/great/ideal classifications.

checks.label: Campaign exact-match attribution coverage is sufficient for campaign-level CPA decisions
notes: Below threshold means campaign tables are still directional for a subset only.

checks.label: Ideal-member sample size is decision-grade
notes: Below threshold: use ideal-member metrics as directional/planning only.

metrics.label: Qualified Lead ($250k-$999,999 official + >1y sober at lead date)
Great Lead (>= $1M official + >1y sober at lead date)

free_events_summary.window_type: Trailing 4 cohort weeks vs prior 4 cohort weeks

drilldown.formula: Total blended spend across all cohort weeks / total paid-social leads across all cohort weeks
notes: Historical benchmark only. Useful for context, not for current Meta decisions.

drilldown.formula: Blended spend in last 4 cohort weeks / paid-social leads created in those same 4 cohort weeks
notes: This is the most useful near-term entry-cost number for budget decisions.

drilldown.formula: Blended spend in last 12 cohort weeks / paid-social leads created in those same 12 cohort weeks
notes: Use this to separate short-term spikes from a genuine pricing regime shift.

drilldown.formula: Spend across matured quality cohorts / qualified lead conversions in those cohorts
notes: Quality cohorts use a 28 day stabilization buffer.

drilldown.formula: Trailing 4-week CPL / finalized qualified-lead conversion rate
notes: This estimates what today’s lead cost implies for qualified leads if conversion quality remains similar.

drilldown.formula: Trailing 4-week CPL / finalized great-lead conversion rate

trend_comparison.label: Forecast cost / great lead vs finalized

drilldown.formula: Trailing 4-week CPL / finalized first-show-up conversion rate
Trailing 4-week CPL / finalized great-member conversion rate
notes: Low sample size. Directional until more great-member outcomes accrue.

drilldown.formula: Trailing 4-week CPL / finalized ideal-member conversion rate
notes: Very sparse sample. Treat as directional / planning-only.

ai_analysis.summary: CPL is materially above the historical benchmark and the downstream forecast costs (especially qualified leads / great leads) are also rising, so this needs active optimization, not passive monitoring.
observations: Trailing 4-week CPL is 10.57 vs blended cohort CPL 6.16 (72% higher).
Trailing 12-week CPL is 8.06; compare this to the 4-week CPL to determine whether the recent rise is a spike or a sustained regime shift.
Current-entry forecast CPQL is 66.7 vs finalized CPQL 36.2 (84% higher).
Current-entry forecast cost per great lead is 84.75 vs finalized 46.
action_steps: Launch a fresh creative batch this week (new hooks/angles + updated visual treatments) and run a clean A/B test against the current winners rather than tweaking small details only.
Evaluate results using CPL and CPQL/CPGL together: if CPL rises but CPQL stays stable, optimize but do not panic; if both rise, rework targeting/creative more aggressively.

Review form quality signals on the top-spend campaigns (especially malformed revenue answers / weird submissions) and confirm qualification copy is clear enough to filter low-fit leads without killing volume.
Use the campaign/ad-set view (as attribution coverage improves) to protect higher-CPQL winners even if their raw CPL is above average.
watch_items: Trailing 4-week CPL vs trailing 12-week CPL gap
Forecast CPQL vs finalized CPQL ratio
Forecast cost / great lead vs finalized cost / great lead ratio
Great-member and ideal-member sample size (still limited, directional only)

drilldown.formula: Paid-social cohort leads with non-generic HubSpot campaign detail that exactly normalized-match a Meta campaign_name in the same lead cohort week / all paid-social cohort leads
notes: Campaign CPA rows are decision-grade only for the exact-match subset. Ad set diagnostics are not yet supported with current attribution fields.

cards.label: Top Campaign by Exact-Matched Leads: ai raised budget 6.93. turn off 1-1 to not cannibalize

drilldown.formula: Exact-matched leads attributed to this campaign across matched campaign-week rows

cards.label: Best Campaign CPQL (Exact Subset): ai raised budget 6.93. turn off 1-1 to not cannibalize

drilldown.formula: Matched campaign-week spend for this campaign / qualified leads from this campaign (exact-match subset)
notes: Use this to protect efficient winners even if raw CPL is not the lowest.

cards.label: High CPL but Strong Quality: (1-3) test 11/3 raised budget (1-1)

drilldown.formula: Matched campaign-week spend / exact-matched leads for this campaign
notes: Qualified lead rate 30.8% is at/above finalized benchmark 15.9%. Consider protecting this campaign while testing cheaper variants.

recommended_actions: Protect and scale-test "ai raised budget 6.93. turn off 1-1 to not cannibalize" as a quality benchmark (best exact-subset CPQL in current snapshot).
Use the campaign table to flag high-CPL + low-quality campaigns once enough exact-match coverage exists.
Do not cut "(1-3) test 11/3 raised budget (1-1)" solely because CPL is high; compare CPQL/CPGL and member outcomes before pausing.
notes: Campaign metrics use exact normalized HubSpot campaign detail -> Meta campaign_name matches in the same lead cohort week when available.
Rows without exact campaign+week match stay visible for outcome rates, but spend-based campaign CPA fields remain null for those rows.
Ad set diagnostics require persistent first-touch ad set IDs/names on contacts and are not yet decision-grade.
